You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Artist. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects describing an artist (or related entity)
    into a single fluent English sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Main entity is the subject of the first triple
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Determine the genre(s) of the main subject, if any
    main_genres = [t.object.strip('"') for t in triples
                   if t.subject == main_subject and t.predicate == "genre"]

    # Helper to turn a predicate/object into a natural clause.
    # Returns either a phrase (to be attached to main_subject) or a full sentence.
    def clause(t: Triple):
        obj = t.object.strip('"')
        p = t.predicate
        # ---------- phrases that belong to the main subject ----------
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            if p in ("associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist",):
                # Use a more natural phrasing for band association
                return f"plays with {obj}"
            if p == "background":
                return f"is a {obj}"
            if p == "genre":
                # Avoid duplicate “music” if the genre already contains the word
                genre_phrase = obj.lower()
                if not genre_phrase.endswith("music"):
                    genre_phrase += " music"
                return f"performs {genre_phrase}"
            if p == "instrument":
                return f"plays the {obj}"
            if p == "origin":
                return f"is from {obj}"
            if p == "birthDate":
                return f"was born on {obj}"
            if p == "birthPlace":
                return f"was born in {obj}"
            if p == "birthYear":
                return f"was born in {obj}"
            if p == "occupation":
                return f"is a {obj}"
            if p == "recordLabel":
                return f"is signed to {obj}"
            if p == "deathPlace":
                return f"died in {obj}"
            if p == "deathDate":
                return f"died on {obj}"
            if p == "nationality":
                return f"is {obj}"
            if p == "professionalField":
                return f"works in {obj}"
            if p in ("language", "officialLanguage"):
                return f"speaks {obj}"
            if p == "capital":
                return f"has capital {obj}"
            if p == "currency":
                return f"uses the {obj}"
            if p == "populationDensity":
                return f"has a population density of {obj}"
            if p == "activeYearsStartYear":
                return f"started performing in {obj}"
            if p == "activeYearsEndYear":
                return f"ended performing in {obj}"
            # default fallback for main subject
            pred_readable = p.replace("_", " ")
            return f"{pred_readable} {obj}"
        # ---------- phrases that relate to the main subject's genre ----------
        if t.subject in main_genres and p in ("stylisticOrigin", "derivative"):
            if p == "stylisticOrigin":
                return f"originated from {obj.lower()}"
            else:  # derivative
                return f"gave rise to {obj.lower()}"
        # ---------- full sentences for secondary subjects ----------
        else:
            if p == "parentCompany":
                return f"{obj} is the parent company of {t.subject}"
            if p == "musicFusionGenre":
                # subject is a genre, object is the fusion genre
                return f"{obj} is a fusion genre that incorporates {t.subject.lower()}"
            if p == "derivative":
                return f"{obj} is derived from {t.subject.lower()}"
            if p == "bandMember":
                # subject is a band, object is a member
                return f"{obj} is a member of {t.subject}"
            if p == "capital":
                return f"The capital of {t.subject} is {obj}"
            if p == "currency":
                return f"The currency of {t.subject} is {obj}"
            if p == "anthem":
                return f"The anthem of {t.subject} is {obj}"
            if p == "demonym":
                return f"The demonym for {t.subject} is {obj}"
            if p == "language" or p == "officialLanguage":
                return f"The official language of {t.subject} is {obj}"
            # default fallback for secondary subjects
            pred_readable = p.replace("_", " ")
            return f"The {pred_readable} of {t.subject} is {obj}"

    # Aggregate predicates for the main subject to allow proper conjunctions
    from collections import defaultdict
    pred_objects = defaultdict(list)          # predicate -> list of objects for main subject
    other_sentences = []                     # sentences for secondary subjects or special cases

    for t in triples:
        obj = t.object.strip('"')
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            pred_objects[t.predicate].append(obj)
        elif t.subject in main_genres and t.predicate in ("stylisticOrigin", "derivative"):
            # treat genre‑related predicates as main‑subject phrases
            pred_objects[t.predicate].append(obj)
        else:
            other_sentences.append(clause(t))

    # Build phrase list for the main subject
    phrase_list = []
    for p, objs in pred_objects.items():
        if p == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
            if len(objs) == 1:
                phrase_list.append(f"is associated with {objs[0]}")
            else:
                phrase_list.append(f"is associated with {', '.join(objs[:-1])} and {objs[-1]}")
        elif p == "genre":
            phrase_list.append(f"performs {', '.join([g.lower() for g in objs])} music")
        elif p == "instrument":
            phrase_list.append(f"plays the {', '.join(objs)}")
        elif p == "origin":
            phrase_list.append(f"is from {', '.join(objs)}")
        elif p == "background":
            phrase_list.append(f"is a {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "birthDate":
            phrase_list.append(f"was born on {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "birthPlace":
            phrase_list.append(f"was born in {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "birthYear":
            phrase_list.append(f"was born in {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "occupation":
            phrase_list.append(f"is a {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "recordLabel":
            phrase_list.append(f"is signed to {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "deathPlace":
            phrase_list.append(f"died in {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "deathDate":
            phrase_list.append(f"died on {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "nationality":
            phrase_list.append(f"is {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "professionalField":
            phrase_list.append(f"works in {objs[0]}")
        elif p in ("language", "officialLanguage"):
            phrase_list.append(f"speaks {objs[0]}")
        elif p == "stylisticOrigin":
            phrase_list.append(f"originated from {', '.join([o.lower() for o in objs])}")
        elif p == "derivative":
            phrase_list.append(f"gave rise to {', '.join([o.lower() for o in objs])}")
        else:
            # fallback for any other predicate
            phrase_list.append(f"{p.replace('_', ' ')} {objs[0]}")

    # Construct the main sentence
    if phrase_list:
        if len(phrase_list) == 1:
            body = phrase_list[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(phrase_list[:-1]) + ", and " + phrase_list[-1]
        main_sentence = f"{main_subject} {body}."
        sentences = [main_sentence] + other_sentences
    else:
        sentences = other_sentences

    # Return the combined text
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
